goal: Investigate artifact tree to verify that propagator, filter, and transformer artifacts are being properly emitted and parented in the trie structure. Validate orphan handling, null output processing, event persistence, and deduplication.

keyFindings: Orphan artifact buffering mechanism verified and working
Null output graceful handling implemented across all execution services
Event-driven persistence chain verified from emission through database
Merge validation confirms clean state with 18 synchronized submodules

planningAgentRequests.scope: Comprehensive Artifact Tree Validation and Test Coverage Planning
planningAgentRequests.goal: Based on the discovery findings of the artifact tree integrity (orphan handling, null output, persistence, deduplication), develop a comprehensive validation and test coverage plan. Focus on: (1) End-to-end integration tests validating propagation artifact emission and parenting, (2) Filter artifact emission and proper event persistence verification, (3) Transformer artifact parent-child relationship establishment and validation, (4) Null output edge case handling across all execution paths, (5) Orphan artifact buffering and eventual parent linking validation, (6) Content-hash deduplication correctness at scale.

expectedOutput.testPlan: Detailed test matrix covering all artifact emission scenarios
expectedOutput.validationStrategy: How to validate that artifacts are properly persisted and linked
expectedOutput.coverageTargets: Test coverage goals for artifact tree layers
expectedOutput.implementationSteps: Step-by-step plan to implement validation tests

planningAgentRequests.rationale: The discovery phase confirmed all architectural components are in place and working. The next critical step is to ensure comprehensive test coverage validates that these components work correctly in all scenarios (happy path, edge cases, null outputs, orphaned artifacts, deduplication). This prevents future regressions and provides confidence in the artifact tree integrity.

fanOutRationale.reason: Single Planning Request - The investigation scope is unified around artifact tree validation. All the verification requirements (orphan handling, null output, persistence, deduplication) are architectural concerns within a single test/validation strategy. No independent implementation tracks identified. A single planning agent can comprehensively decompose the validation requirements.

expectedPlanningOutput.structure: Detailed test plan with specific test cases for each artifact tree component
scope: Propagation artifact emission and event persistence validation
Null output handling across execution services
Orphan artifact buffering and eventual linking
expectedPlanningOutput.nextPhase: IMPLEMENTATION - Execute the test plan and validation strategy
